topic: The Most Powerful Engine on Earth

beats.narration: From two hundred miles up, it looks almost gentle. A slow white pinwheel, turning over the sea. It is the most powerful engine on Earth.
beats.intended_understanding: establish the storm as deceptively serene from orbit before the mechanism reveal

beats.narration: It is born from a single thing. Warm ocean, above twenty-six degrees Celsius, breathing heat and water into the sky.
beats.intended_understanding: the one ingredient a hurricane needs — a warm sea surface

beats.narration: That warm, wet air rises. High up it cools and rains, and every drop that condenses releases heat, pulling more air upward behind it. The storm becomes its own engine, feeding on the sea.
beats.intended_understanding: the self-feeding heat-engine feedback loop — the mechanism at the heart of the film

beats.narration: We rank them by their winds — from a category one, to a five past two hundred and fifty, strong enough to erase a coastline.
beats.intended_understanding: a BRIEF wind-scale check (kept short so it never becomes an infographic beat), landing the top-of-scale number before the reveal

beats.narration: And at the very center, calm. A ring of towering cloud around an eye of perfect stillness, circled by winds strong enough to release the energy of ten thousand bombs a day.
beats.intended_understanding: the showpiece — the eye, the emotional and visual peak, carrying the power number

beats.narration: Warm water and air. That is all it is. And once it begins to turn, nothing on Earth can stop it.
beats.intended_understanding: thesis — the storm is a simple machine of overwhelming power; a true bookend that returns to the exact opening frame (callback); the film ENDS here
